prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, sweetie, a CPU is like the brain of your computer. It's the part that does all the thinking and makes your computer work. Without it, your computer wouldn't be able to do anything at all. It's in charge of taking all the information and making it into something we can use on our screens. It's a very important part of the computer.
